intentional_test_features: Full decompensated-heart-failure tetrad (orthopnoea/PND, elevated JVP, S3 gallop, bilateral oedema) with resting hypoxaemia - must be recognised as acute pulmonary oedema needing urgent treatment, not a routine review

Clear precipitant - missed diuretic doses from a facility prescription-refill error: the AI Doctor should identify medication non-compliance as the reversible driver and ensure the diuretic is restarted and the refill error fixed

Aged-care telehealth-video context: nursing staff can relay observations/vitals and administer prescribed measures, but definitive IV diuresis and monitoring for possible non-invasive ventilation require in-person medical assessment
Resting hypoxaemia (SpO2 91% on room air) with accessory-muscle use indicates significant pulmonary oedema and impending respiratory failure - a low threshold for emergency transfer
High fall risk from leg heaviness and nocturnal toileting must be addressed alongside the cardiac emergency

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
